rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical alkoxycarbonyl-alkoxylation of styrenes with potassium ethyl oxalate and alcohols via a radical-polar crossover pathway
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (from NH4I mediator) generates I2/I• which oxidatively decarboxylates the oxalate to an ethoxycarbonyl radical; this carbon radical adds to the styrene and the resulting benzylic radical is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation trapped by EtOH

key_intermediates.name: ethoxycarbonyl radical (•CO2Et) from decarboxylation of ethyl oxalate
benzylic C-radical after addition to styrene
benzylic carbocation after anodic oxidation of benzyl radical
cyclopropylcarbinyl radical / ring-opened homoallyl radical (from radical clock substrate 13)

electron_flow: Anodic oxidation of iodide regenerates an I-based mediator that oxidizes potassium ethyl oxalate, releasing CO2 and an ethoxycarbonyl radical; this radical adds to the styrene to give a benzylic radical, which is oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation and trapped by ethanol, while the cathode reduces protons/NH4+ to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Addition of 2 equiv TEMPO completely suppressed formation of product 3a (no 3a detected by GC-MS/1H NMR), consistent with a radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: (1-cyclopropylvinyl)-4-methylbenzene (radical clock substrate 13)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave both the unopened cyclopropyl product 14 (63%) and the ring-opened homoallylic ether 15 (10%), indicating a carbon-centered radical intermediate that partially undergoes cyclopropylcarbinyl ring opening

notes: Mechanistic studies shown include only TEMPO trapping and a radical clock; the radical-polar crossover (oxidation of benzylic radical to cation, trapped by alcohol) is inferred from the alkoxylation outcome rather than directly probed on these pages. NH4I acts as a redox mediator and Et4NBF4 as supporting electrolyte.
